ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn entry for durian polo bun; closest parent is generic bun food product.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (sweet buns and rolls) is the closest defensible parent; no specific code for durian-filled polo bun exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for durian polo bun or sufficiently close parent concept in clinical terminology.

serving.natural_language_portion: One durian polo bun piece weighing approximately 90 g, as typically sold at a Singapore bakery or café.

nutrient_source.title: Polo Bun (Pineapple Bun) with Sweet Filling — estimated from USDA SR Legacy bread/bun components and HPB Singapore Foodo durian flesh data
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service (SR Legacy) and Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for durian polo bun exists in HPB Foodo or USDA databases. Values estimated by combining: (1) USDA SR Legacy sweet bread/bun base (USDA FDC ID 18029, sweet rolls/buns) for the dough and cookie-crust topping, and (2) HPB Singapore Foodo durian flesh nutrient data for the filling component. Proportions estimated as approximately 70% bun dough/crust and 30% durian filling by weight. Energy, macros, and secondary nutrients derived from weighted average of these two components.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of white bread and sweet bun products — analogous estimate based on sweet white bread roll GI data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for durian polo bun. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on University of Sydney GI Database data for sweet white bread rolls and enriched bun products (GI range 63–70). Durian flesh has a reported GI of approximately 49–58 (low-medium); the dominant dough component drives the composite GI upward. Weighted estimate of 65 is conservative and defensible. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 44.6) / 100 ≈ 29.0, rounded to 29.2 using 90 g serving size (44.55 g carbohydrate per serving).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content and refined carbohydrate load
hypertension.guidance: Each durian polo bun provides approximately 189 mg sodium per serving; individuals managing hypertension should limit intake to one piece and avoid pairing with other high-sodium foods in the same meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load and significant sugar content
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycemic load of approximately 29 per serving and 14.4 g of sugar per piece, this bun causes a substantial postprandial glucose rise and should be consumed only occasionally and in small portions by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and refined sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each serving contributes approximately 4.1 g saturated fat and 14.4 g sugar; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption and choose lower-fat, lower-sugar bakery alternatives where possible.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Durian Polo Bun (榴莲菠萝包) is a popular Singaporean and Hong Kong-inspired bakery item that combines the classic polo bun (菠萝包, pineapple bun) format with a rich, creamy durian filling. The polo bun is characterised by its soft, pillowy bread interior and a distinctive crumbly, sweet cookie-dough crust on top that is scored in a crosshatch pattern resembling a pineapple skin — hence the name — though it contains no actual pineapple. In the durian variant, the bun is filled with a generous portion of fresh or frozen durian flesh or durian paste, which imparts the fruit's intensely sweet, custardy, and pungent flavour profile. The aroma is strong and distinctive, making this item immediately recognisable. Durian polo buns are sold at specialist durian dessert shops, Hong Kong-style cafés (茶餐厅, cha chaan teng), and bakeries across Singapore, and have gained popularity as a premium seasonal or signature item. Nutritionally, the bun is energy-dense at approximately 310 kcal per 100 g, with a moderate protein content of 6.2 g, significant carbohydrate load of 49.5 g, and fat of 9.8 g per 100 g. The sugar content is notable at 16 g per 100 g, and the glycemic load per standard serving (90 g) is high at approximately 29, making this a food of concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular risk. The durian filling contributes dietary fibre, potassium, and vitamin C, partially offsetting the refined carbohydrate base. This item is suitable for vegetarians and is generally halal-certified in Singapore bakery contexts, though consumers should verify allergen information as it contains gluten, egg, and dairy.
common_user_queries: What is a durian polo bun?
How many calories in a durian polo bun?
Can diabetics eat durian polo bun?
Where to buy durian polo bun in Singapore?
What is the GI of durian polo bun?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for durian polo bun exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA databases. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted combination of USDA SR Legacy sweet bun data and HPB Foodo durian flesh data. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database sweet bread roll data. Confidence is medium because the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible but not based on direct laboratory analysis of this specific product.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in either the USDA SR Legacy sweet bun source or the HPB Foodo durian flesh data and cannot be reliably estimated for this composite product. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary sources used for this estimation. All other -1 values reflect genuine absence of data in the approved sources consulted. Nutrient values are per 100 g of the composite product (approximately 70% polo bun dough/crust, 30% durian filling by weight). Serving size of 90 g reflects a typical single polo bun piece as sold at Singapore bakeries. The food_name has been normalised from the input 'Premium Durian Polo x 1pc' to the canonical English name 'Durian Polo Bun'. The input food_group 'Mixed' has been reclassified to 'Snacks' as this is a sweet filled bun product. Halal status is assumed based on typical Singapore bakery practice but consumers should verify with the specific vendor (Kazo Premium - Funan). GI of 65 is a medium-category analogous estimate; direct GI testing of this product has not been identified in the literature.
